title: tanacetum argyrophyllum var. subvirescens
intro: Tanacetum argyrophyllum var. subvirescens, also known as the Tansy-leaved mountain sage, is a variety of plant that belongs to the genus Tanacetum and is native to certain regions. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Tanacetum argyrophyllum var. subvirescens include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve headaches and fever, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms.
